story.iWant: to add the `resultMode` parameter to the `/mcp/search` endpoint schema with validation
story.soThat: clients can specify which level of detail they need in search results (minimal, snippets, or full)
story.tasks: - Task 1: Update TypeScript Type Definitions (5 subtasks) - Task 2: Implement Request Validation Middleware (7 subtasks) - Task 3: Update Search Endpoint Handler (6 subtasks) - Task 4: Add Error Handling Tests (5 subtasks) - Task 5: Integration Testing and Backward Compatibility (7 subtasks) - Task 6: Update OpenAPI Specification (7 subtasks) - Task 7: Deploy and Smoke Test (7 subtasks)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**AC-1: API Parameter Handling** - API accepts resultMode values: "minimal", "snippets", "full" (case-sensitive) - Defaults to "snippets" if parameter omitted - Returns 400 Bad Request for invalid resultMode values with clear error message
**AC-2: Error Response Format** - Error response includes: {"error": {"code": "INVALID_RESULT_MODE", "message": "resultMode must be one of: minimal, snippets, full", "allowed_values": ["minimal", "snippets", "full"]}} - HTTP status code is 400 Bad Request - Error response follows MCP v2 protocol format
**AC-3: Response Mode Echo** - Response includes `mode` field indicating which mode was used - `mode` field matches requested resultMode (or "snippets" if omitted) - `mode` field included in all successful responses
**AC-4: Backward Compatibility** - Existing clients without resultMode parameter continue working - API defaults to "snippets" mode (current production behavior) - No breaking changes to existing response structure - All existing clients work without modifications

docs.doc: Defines the `resultMode` parameter schema, validation rules, error response format, and traceability mapping. Specifies enum values (minimal, snippets, full), default value ("snippets"), and backward compatibility requirements. Includes TypeScript interfaces for MinimalResult, SnippetResult, FullResult.

doc.title: PRD Enhancement: MCP API Result Modes

docs.doc: Describes the motivation for result modes (AI assistants need different detail levels, performance optimization, bandwidth control). Defines three result modes with use cases and example responses. Specifies performance targets: minimal <500ms, snippets <1500ms, full <3000ms.
Current API schema documentation. Will be extended with resultMode parameter definition, enum constraint, default value documentation, and error response examples for invalid modes.

file.reason: Main search endpoint handler - will be modified to extract and validate resultMode parameter, pass to service layer, and echo mode in response

code.file: Current implementation: Validates query and limit parameters, calls VertexSearchService, returns results with metadata. Contains inline type definitions (MCPSearchRequest, MCPErrorResponse) that should be extracted to separate types file.

file.reason: Request interface to be extended with resultMode field

code.file: Current definition: interface MCPSearchRequest { query: string; limit?: number; }. Will add: resultMode?: 'minimal' | 'snippets' | 'full';

file.reason: Error response interface - used for validation errors

code.file: Defines MCP v2 error format: { error: { code: string; message: string; details?: any; } }. Already correct format for INVALID_RESULT_MODE errors.

file.reason: Search service implementation - will be modified to accept resultMode parameter (no logic changes in this story)

code.file: Current signature: async search(options: SearchOptions): Promise<SearchResult[]>. Will extend SearchOptions to include resultMode (for stories 8.2-8.4 to use).

file.reason: Service options interface to be extended with resultMode

code.file: Current definition: interface SearchOptions { query: string; limit?: number; }. Will add: resultMode?: 'minimal' | 'snippets' | 'full';

file.reason: Result interface used by all modes

code.file: Base result structure with title, url, snippet, metadata. Serves as baseline for all three result modes.
Current route: app.post("/mcp/search", search). No changes needed, but good to understand Express middleware flow.

file.reason: Global error handling middleware - reference for consistent error responses

code.file: Handles unhandled errors. Validation errors will be caught earlier in request validation middleware, but this provides pattern reference.

file.reason: Existing integration tests - will be extended with resultMode test cases

code.file: Current tests validate search functionality. Will add tests for: valid resultMode values, invalid values, omitted parameter (default behavior), mode echo in response.

node.dependency: HTTP server framework - used for routing and middleware
Testing framework for unit and integration tests
HTTP testing library for API integration tests

story-context.constraints: - **TypeScript Strict Mode**: All code must compile with strict: true (noUnusedLocals, noImplicitReturns, etc.) - **Backward Compatibility**: CRITICAL - existing clients without resultMode parameter must continue working unchanged - **MCP v2 Protocol**: Error responses must follow MCP v2 format: { error: { code, message, [details] } } - **Enum Validation**: resultMode must be validated as one of three exact strings: "minimal", "snippets", "full" (case-sensitive) - **Default Value**: When resultMode is omitted or undefined, must default to "snippets" (maintains current behavior) - **No Breaking Changes**: Response structure must remain compatible - mode field is additive - **Validation at Boundary**: Validate resultMode in middleware/controller before passing to service layer - **Error Message Clarity**: Invalid enum errors must include allowed_values array for developer clarity - **Structured Logging**: Log resultMode usage for analytics (query success logs should include mode field)

interface.name: MCPSearchRequest
interface.signature: interface MCPSearchRequest { query: string; limit?: number; resultMode?: 'minimal' | 'snippets' | 'full'; }

interfaces.interface: Request schema for /mcp/search endpoint. Will be extracted from inline definition in searchController.ts to new types file.

interface.name: MCPSearchResponse
interface.signature: interface MCPSearchResponse { results: SearchResult[]; metadata: { query: string; limit: number; resultCount: number; duration: number; }; mode: 'minimal' | 'snippets' | 'full'; }

interfaces.interface: Response schema with new mode field. Currently response is inline in searchController.ts, will be formalized in types file.

interface.name: MCPErrorResponse
interface.signature: interface MCPErrorResponse { error: { code: string; message: string; details?: any; allowed_values?: string[]; } }

interfaces.interface: Error response schema. Extended to include allowed_values for INVALID_RESULT_MODE errors.

interface.name: POST /mcp/search
interface.signature: POST /mcp/search { query: string, limit?: number, resultMode?: 'minimal' | 'snippets' | 'full' } → { results: SearchResult[], metadata: {...}, mode: string }

interfaces.interface: Main search endpoint. Accepts resultMode parameter (optional, defaults to "snippets"). Returns results with mode echo field.

interface.name: validateRequest
interface.signature: (req: Request, res: Response, next: NextFunction) => void

interfaces.interface: Request validation middleware to be created. Validates resultMode enum, sets default value, returns 400 error for invalid values.

tests.standards: Testing framework: Vitest (configured in package.json). Test locations follow pattern: api/test/unit/*.test.ts for unit tests, api/test/integration/*.test.ts for integration tests. TypeScript strict mode applies to test files. Integration tests use supertest for HTTP testing. Test commands: npm test (all tests), npm run test:integration (integration only). Coverage not currently configured but recommended for new code.
tests.locations: - api/test/unit/ - Unit tests for validation logic and helpers - api/test/integration/ - Integration tests for full request/response cycle - api/test/validation.test.ts - New file for resultMode validation unit tests - api/test/integration/resultMode.test.ts - New file for resultMode integration tests
tests.ideas:
**AC-1 Tests (API Parameter Handling):** - Test valid resultMode: "minimal" → accepts and processes - Test valid resultMode: "snippets" → accepts and processes - Test valid resultMode: "full" → accepts and processes - Test omitted resultMode → defaults to "snippets" - Test invalid resultMode: "invalid" → returns 400 INVALID_RESULT_MODE - Test case sensitivity: "MINIMAL" → returns 400 (not "minimal") - Test invalid types: null, 123, {}, [] → all return 400
**AC-2 Tests (Error Response Format):** - Verify error response structure matches: { error: { code, message, allowed_values } } - Verify code is "INVALID_RESULT_MODE" - Verify message is clear and actionable - Verify allowed_values array contains ["minimal", "snippets", "full"] - Verify HTTP status code is 400
**AC-3 Tests (Response Mode Echo):** - Request with resultMode: "minimal" → response.mode === "minimal" - Request with resultMode: "snippets" → response.mode === "snippets" - Request with resultMode: "full" → response.mode === "full" - Request without resultMode → response.mode === "snippets" - Verify mode field present in all successful responses
**AC-4 Tests (Backward Compatibility):** - Test existing request format (query only) → still works, gets snippets mode - Test existing request format (query + limit) → still works, gets snippets mode - Run full existing test suite → all tests pass without modification - Verify no breaking changes to response structure (results, metadata still present)
